$comment: Default US tax ruleset — the generic, shareable line catalog the mapping engine (issue #23) maps YNAB payees/categories ONTO, and the default standardDeductionByYear + thresholds a tax profile (assets/tax/tax-profile.schema.json, issue #20) falls back to when the user supplies no override. PURELY DECLARATIVE: this file is data only — no JavaScript, no template expressions, no computed fields, no executable logic. NOTHING here is specific to one taxpayer; per-user numbers (which businesses exist, override amounts) live in the profile INSTANCE (issue #25), never here. MONEY UNITS: every monetary amount in this file is in US DOLLARS, not YNAB milliunits (divide YNAB milliunits by 1000 to get dollars); each dollar field's description restates this. RATES are fractions (0.075 = 7.5%), not dollars and not percentages. NO vendor keyword lists or payee/category heuristics appear here — those are the mapping engine's concern (issue #23). The id scheme and field names are kept consistent with the tax-profile schema (issue #20): Schedule A line ids match its itemized.<bucket> keys, Schedule 1 line ids match its adjustments.<key> keys, filing-status keys match its FilingStatus enum, and the thresholds keys mirror its thresholds object. IRS line numbers are cited as they appear in the workbench-ynab prototype SKILL.md (Schedule C lines 29-41, Schedule A 42-63, Schedule 1 64-67, Schedule SE 69-70).

lines.description: Total business income received before expenses — the top-line revenue for a sole-proprietorship / pass-through business. Gross receipts, sales, and fees collected belong here.
Advertising and marketing costs to promote the business.
Commissions and fees paid to non-employees for business services (excludes contract labor reported on line 11).
Payments to independent contractors for services performed for the business (amounts that would be reported on a Form 1099-NEC).
Depreciation of business property and any section 179 expensing election for the year.
Fees for legal, accounting, bookkeeping, and other professional services used by the business.
Ordinary office operating costs such as office supplies and postage (distinct from supplies on line 22 and utilities on line 25).
Cost of supplies and materials consumed in the business that are not part of cost of goods sold.
Utilities used for the business such as electricity, gas, water, and business phone/internet service.

Catch-all for ordinary and necessary business expenses that do not fit a named Schedule C line (for example software and subscription costs). The specific payee/category rules that route transactions here live in the mapping engine (issue #23), not in this catalog.

Schedule A itemized medical and dental expenses. Only the portion above thresholds.medicalAgiPercent of AGI is deductible; the threshold itself lives under thresholds, not on this record. Household-level, not tied to a business entity.

Schedule A state-and-local taxes: income or sales tax plus property tax, deductible up to thresholds.saltCap dollars (federal cap is 10000, halved to 5000 if filing MFS — applied by filing status downstream by the engine, not in this catalog). Household-level, not tied to a business entity.
Schedule A deductible interest: qualified home mortgage interest and HELOC interest. Household-level, not tied to a business entity.
Schedule A charitable contributions: cash and non-cash gifts to qualified organizations. Household-level, not tied to a business entity.

Schedule 1 adjustment for one-half of self-employment tax. This amount is derived from the computed SE tax (thresholds.seTaxRate) by the engine; the catalog only declares that the line exists. Household-level adjustment to income.
Schedule 1 above-the-line deduction for student loan interest paid, subject to the IRS annual cap and income phase-out applied by the engine. Household-level adjustment to income.
Schedule 1 above-the-line deduction for deductible traditional-IRA contributions. Household-level adjustment to income.

Schedule SE self-employment tax on net earnings from self-employment, combining 12.4% Social Security and 2.9% Medicare for a 15.3% combined rate (see rate, which mirrors thresholds.seTaxRate — the tunable source of truth). Household-level; one-half of the computed tax is deductible via sched1.seTaxHalfDeduction.

standardDeductionByYear.$comment: Standard deduction lookup keyed by filing status, then by four-digit tax-year string, mirroring standardDeductionByYear in tax-profile.schema.json (issue #20). Every value is the standard deduction in US DOLLARS, not YNAB milliunits (divide YNAB milliunits by 1000 to get dollars). Filing-status keys: single = Single; mfj = Married Filing Jointly; mfs = Married Filing Separately; hoh = Head of Household; qw = Qualifying Surviving Spouse. Adding a new tax year is a pure data edit: add a new four-digit year key with its dollar amount under each filing status — no code change.

thresholds.$comment: Tunable tax thresholds and rates, mirroring the thresholds object in tax-profile.schema.json (issue #20). Rates are fractions (0.075 = 7.5%), not dollars and not percentages; saltCap is a US DOLLAR amount, not YNAB milliunits (divide YNAB milliunits by 1000 to get dollars). The federal saltCap is 10000 (5000 if filing MFS); this single scalar is the base cap, and the MFS halving is applied by filing status downstream by the engine, not encoded here.
